explanation:
The requirement states that if 'New Patient' is selected, (1) the user must enter patient attributes, and (2) every step of the self-test procedure must be completed (i.e., no skipping or failing of required steps). Given the available atomic propositions, we have:
- 'newPatient' indicates 'New Patient' selected.
- 'patientAttributesEntered' indicates patient attributes are entered.
- 'selfTestPass' indicates all self-test steps passed successfully (i.e., procedure completed).

To express that whenever 'newPatient' is selected, entering patient attributes and successful self-test completion are both mandatory before proceeding further, we express this as: 
Globally, if 'newPatient' is true, then eventually both 'patientAttributesEntered' and 'selfTestPass' must be achieved (i.e., both must happen in the future after 'newPatient').

LTL: G ( newPatient -> (F patientAttributesEntered & F selfTestPass) )

This accurately captures the requirement's mandatory progression. Previous attempts at expressing the requirement may have missed that BOTH entering patient attributes and self-test success must hold after 'newPatient' is selected, or may have used only one direction of implication ('if' versus 'only if'), or failed to ensure both conditions eventually occur.

The requirement states that if 'New Patient' is selected, then (i) the user must enter patient attributes, and (ii) every step of the self-test procedure must be completed (i.e., made mandatory). From the available atomic propositions, 'newPatient' captures the selection event, 'patientAttributesEntered' indicates the attributes are filled in, and 'selfTestPass' indicates all self-test steps passed successfully (which is the best approximation in the absence of specific atomic props for each self-test step). The property is best captured as a global constraint: whenever 'newPatient' occurs, eventually both 'patientAttributesEntered' and 'selfTestPass' must also occur. So this is written using the LTL implication and conjunction: G(newPatient -> (F patientAttributesEntered & F selfTestPass)).

Alternate LTL expressions (like just F(patientAttributesEntered & selfTestPass)) would not capture the dependency on 'newPatient', and using X (next) would be too strict, as order/steps may need some user time. The implication with global (G) and eventually (F) operators best fits the requirement given the available props.

The natural language states that if 'New Patient' is selected, the user shall be required to enter patient attributes and must complete every step of the self-test procedure. From the provided atomic propositions, 'newPatient' can be used to represent when 'New Patient' is selected, 'patientAttributesEntered' indicates patient attributes have been entered, and 'selfTestPass' indicates all self-test steps have been passed successfully. To capture the requirement that both of these are mandatory after 'newPatient' is selected, the property states that whenever 'newPatient' occurs, it must be inevitably followed (eventually) by both 'patientAttributesEntered' and 'selfTestPass'. This is best represented as: G(newPatient -> (F patientAttributesEntered & F selfTestPass)). This guarantees that whenever 'newPatient' is true, both patient attributes will eventually be entered and the self-test will eventually pass, thus making both actions mandatory before proceeding.

G( newPatient -> (F patientAttributesEntered & F selfTestPass) )

captures this, as for every moment that 'newPatient' becomes true, both 'patientAttributesEntered' and 'selfTestPass' must eventually follow. The natural language states: if 'New Patient' is selected, two things must happen: (1) the user must enter patient attributes and (2) all steps of the self-test procedure must be completed (no steps skipped). Based on the atomic propositions available, we capture (1) as 'patientAttributesEntered' and (2) as 'selfTestPass'. While there is no atomic proposition explicitly for 'completion of every step' or for 'steps being mandatory', 'selfTestPass' is the best available indicator that all self-test steps were successfully completed (none skipped or failed). Thus, the LTL property should specify that whenever 'newPatient' holds, both 'patientAttributesEntered' and 'selfTestPass' must eventually happen before anything else (such as starting ventilation or other actions). The property specifies that whenever 'newPatient' is selected, it is required that 'patientAttributesEntered' will eventually happen (i.e., the user must enter patient attributes), and that 'selfTestPass' will eventually happen (i.e., all self-test steps must be completed and passed). This interpretation captures the intent that completion of these actions is mandatory after selecting 'New Patient.' It does not try to model every internal step of the self-test, since only an atomic proposition for overall self-test pass is available. If the logic allowed skipping, an alternate property would reflect that, but here, 'eventually' enforces necessity. In contrast to other possible LTLs that simply ensure these things happen at some point, the use of the implication and 'globally' ensures the requirements only apply when 'newPatient' is selected, not at arbitrary times.

output_LTL: G ( newPatient -> ( F patientAttributesEntered & F selfTestPass ) )

explanation: The natural language states that if 'New Patient' is selected, the user must enter patient attributes and completion of every step of the self-test procedure is mandatory. According to the atomic propositions provided, 'newPatient' indicates 'New Patient' is selected, 'patientAttributesEntered' indicates patient attributes are entered, and 'selfTestPass' indicates all self-test steps passed successfully. To capture 'if New Patient is selected, patient attributes must be entered and every self-test step must be completed,' we use the release (V) operator to ensure that from the selection of 'newPatient' continuing until both 'patientAttributesEntered' and 'selfTestPass' hold, it is required that after 'newPatient', eventually both must hold. The LTL formula 'G(newPatient -> (F(patientAttributesEntered) & F(selfTestPass)))' expresses that globally, whenever 'newPatient' occurs, eventually 'patientAttributesEntered' and 'selfTestPass' must occur. Previous approaches such as 'G(newPatient -> X(patientAttributesEntered & selfTestPass))' would not capture that 'patientAttributesEntered' and 'selfTestPass' could happen some time after 'newPatient', not necessarily immediately in the next step. Thus, the provided property accurately captures the mandatory fulfillment for any occurrence of 'newPatient'.
